name: Lyon, France
description: Quai des Célestins along the Saône River, looking toward Vieux Lyon.

tier1.display: People sitting on walls — hostile architecture

clueHighlights.clue: Plane trees + European blue signs
Cable-stayed pedestrian bridge + vehicle bridge
Separated bike lane + T2 tram + compact cars

frq.prompt: Explain how the transportation infrastructure visible in this image reflects the city's level of economic development.
frq.modelAnswer: The image shows a separated bicycle lane running parallel to a vehicular road and tram line, indicating a city with resources to invest in multi-modal transportation. This level of transit investment — cable-stayed pedestrian bridge, electrified tram, dedicated cycling lanes — is characteristic of a core country where surplus capital supports quality-of-life infrastructure.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Economic and Spatial categories. Practices the Explain task verb requiring causal reasoning.

name: Istanbul, Turkey
description: Karaköy waterfront near Galata Bridge, looking across the Golden Horn.

tier2.display: Dersaadet is Ottoman name for Istanbul

tier1.display: Two-level vertical land use on bridge

clueHighlights.clue: GALATA + DERSAADET on bridge signage
Two-level bridge with tram + restaurants below
Tünel sign visible

frq.prompt: Explain how the site characteristics visible in this image influenced the historical development of this city as a major trade center.
frq.modelAnswer: The image shows a dense urban area at a waterway crossing with a bridge spanning a protected inlet. This site at a natural harbor attracted trade routes, while bridge infrastructure shows ongoing connectivity investment. Water access for maritime trade combined with bridge crossings for overland routes shows how site features at a water confluence create conditions for commercial hub development.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Site and Situation (Topic 6.1) through Environmental and Spatial categories.

name: Nairobi, Kenya — Kibera Area (East)
description: Street near Kibera informal settlement showing market activity and water collection.

tier1.display: No traffic signs or road markings
Yellow water jerry cans (water access issue)

tier2.display: Water scarcity — collecting water in containers
Young population suggests high birth rate

clueHighlights.clue: Yellow water jerry cans + unpaved road
Corrugated metal roofs + small market stalls
Many children + high density on narrow road

frq.prompt: Describe TWO characteristics visible in this image that indicate this is a settlement in a peripheral or semi-peripheral country.
frq.modelAnswer: The unpaved dirt road with no lane markings or traffic signs indicates limited government investment in transportation infrastructure, common in peripheral countries. The corrugated metal roofing on market structures shows reliance on low-cost building materials, reflecting limited capital for permanent construction typical of informal economies.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Economic and Political categories with a Describe prompt. Students practice naming specific visible characteristics without causal reasoning.

name: Nairobi, Kenya — Kibera Area (West)
description: Street near Kibera showing M-PESA mobile money, motorcycle taxis, and market activity.

tier2.display: Visible income disparity — SUV vs motorcycle

clueHighlights.clue: M-PESA sign (green booth)
English signage + unpaved road + corrugated roofs
MILK-ATM + salon + motorcycle taxis

frq.prompt: Explain how the M-PESA mobile money service visible in this image is an example of leapfrog technology in a developing country.
frq.modelAnswer: The M-PESA booth represents leapfrog technology because it allows residents to conduct banking via mobile phones without first building a network of traditional bank branches. Populations in peripheral countries skipped landline telephone and brick-and-mortar banking stages, moving directly to mobile-based financial services, reducing infrastructure investment needed for economic participation.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Economic category connecting to Topic 7.8 (Technology and development). The Explain verb requires causal reasoning behind leapfrog technology.

name: Hong Kong, China
description: Mong Kok district intersection with dense urban canyon, bilingual signage, and British-influenced road design.

tier2.display: British colonial legacy in bilingual signage

tier1.display: No visible vegetation — fully built environment

tier2.display: High land values drive vertical growth
One of highest population densities on Earth

tier1.display: Urban canyon — tall buildings both sides

clueHighlights.clue: Chinese + English bilingual + left-hand traffic
Citibank + Zoff (Japanese brand)
Double-decker bus + red taxi + yellow crosswalks

frq.prompt: Explain how the built environment visible in this image reflects bid-rent theory.
frq.modelAnswer: The image shows extremely tall commercial buildings at an intersection with ground-floor retail and major banks like Citibank. According to bid-rent theory, commercial activities generating the most revenue per square meter outbid other land uses for central locations, driving buildings upward to maximize floor space on expensive land. The high-rise density indicates this is near the urban core where land values are highest.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Spatial and Economic categories connecting to Topic 6.5. Bid-rent theory appears frequently on the AP exam.

name: Mumbai, India
description: Commercial street near Dharavi showing electronics shops, Mumbai taxis, and dense mixed-use development.

tier2.display: English as commercial language — colonial legacy

tier1.display: Informal vehicle parking — no organized lots

tier2.display: South Asian city model — bazaar district

clueHighlights.clue: Hindi + English bilingual signs
Black-and-yellow taxis
Dense motorcycle parking + electronics + jewelry shops

frq.prompt: Compare TWO visible differences between the infrastructure in this image and what you would expect in a core/developed country.
frq.modelAnswer: The minimally maintained road surface with basic center-line markings contrasts with multi-lane paved roads with clear markings, signals, and separated pedestrian infrastructure in core countries. The dense informal motorcycle parking on the roadside differs from organized parking structures in developed cities, reflecting different levels of capital investment in transportation infrastructure.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Economic and Spatial categories with a Compare verb. Students must make explicit comparisons, not parallel descriptions.

name: Detroit, Michigan, USA
description: Michigan Avenue corridor downtown showing Art Deco architecture and urban renewal indicators.

tier2.display: Motor City identity — auto industry history

tier1.display: Art Deco building with columns (1920s prosperity)
Very low pedestrian density for downtown

clueHighlights.clue: FUELING THE MOTOR CITY coffee sign
Art Deco columns + wide empty street
Specialty coffee + e-scooter + renovation

frq.prompt: Explain how evidence of both urban decline AND urban renewal are visible in this image.
frq.modelAnswer: The wide avenue with very few pedestrians and oversized infrastructure indicate population loss from deindustrialization when the auto industry declined. Simultaneously, a specialty coffee shop in a renovated Art Deco building and an electric scooter indicate new investment and gentrification, showing portions of the city center are experiencing reinvestment while the broader core remains underutilized.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Economic and Demographic categories connecting to Topics 6.10-6.11. The Explain verb requires causal reasoning for both decline and renewal.

name: Delhi, India — Chandni Chowk
description: Pedestrianized commercial street in the Chandni Chowk bazaar district, showing Mughal-era heritage signage and recent government redevelopment.

tier1.display: Raymond brand ad with male portrait

tier2.display: Formal retail + informal porter labor coexist
Gender imbalance in public commercial space

clueHighlights.clue: Devanagari script + LEHENGA & SHERWANI signs
Porters with cargo bundles + rolling security gates
Ornate lamp posts + center planters + no cars

frq.prompt: Explain how the visible features in this image reflect BOTH traditional cultural practices AND government urban-renewal investment.
frq.modelAnswer: The image shows Hindu-themed shop names such as Laxmi Hari and ethnic-clothing retail for lehengas and sherwanis, reflecting traditional South Asian cultural practices tied to weddings and religious commerce. At the same time, raised center planters, ornate lamp posts, and a fully pedestrianized street show recent municipal investment in the Chandni Chowk redevelopment, which prioritizes pedestrian access and preserves cultural-landscape character while modernizing the bazaar's infrastructure.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Social (cultural landscape) and Political (urban renewal) categories connecting Topic 3.2 with Topic 6.3. The Explain verb requires connecting traditional culture to contemporary policy intervention.

name: Berlin, Germany — Kottbusser Tor
description: Public plaza at Kottbusser Tor in Kreuzberg showing Turkish market commerce alongside German modernist housing and the elevated U-Bahn.

clueHighlights.clue: "ISTANBUL SUPERMARKT" next to German-language "NACHHILFESCHULE"
Elevated U-Bahn viaduct + modernist mid-rise
Bare deciduous trees + light clothing absent

frq.prompt: Explain how the cultural landscape visible in this image reflects international migration patterns into Germany.
frq.modelAnswer: The image shows a Turkish-brand supermarket next to a German-language tutoring school within the same plaza, reflecting the Turkish guest-worker migration to Germany that began in the 1960s. The presence of Turkish ethnic retail serving daily shopping needs indicates an established immigrant community that has maintained cultural identity through food, language, and commerce, creating a visible multicultural neighborhood fabric that persists across generations.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Social (cultural landscape) and Demographic (migration patterns) categories connecting Topic 3.2 with Topic 2.10. The Explain verb requires linking migration drivers to visible cultural-landscape outcomes.

name: Varanasi, India — Old City
description: Commercial street near the ghats of Varanasi showing Hindu religious commerce, regional paan culture, and global-brand presence.

tier2.display: Marigolds used in Hindu worship (puja offerings)
Paan as South Asian folk-food practice

clueHighlights.clue: Orange marigold garlands + "Banarasi Paan" sign
Coca-Cola sign + Vardhman wool shop + street vendor together
Salmon-pink facades + Devanagari script

frq.prompt: Describe TWO visible characteristics that indicate this street contains BOTH traditional cultural practices AND evidence of globalization.
frq.modelAnswer: Hanging orange marigold garlands and a "Paan Bhandar" (betel-leaf shop) sign with the regional term "Banarasi" show traditional Hindu religious and folk-food practices still embedded in everyday commerce. On the same shopfront, Coca-Cola branding and Latin-alphabet English text show globalization, where an international consumer brand has been integrated into the same vernacular storefront as the traditional products.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Social (cultural landscape) and Economic (globalization) categories connecting Topic 3.2 with Topic 7.8. The Describe verb requires naming specific characteristics without causal reasoning.

name: Cusco, Peru — Plaza de Armas
description: Plaza de Armas showing the Spanish Baroque cathedral built on Inca palace foundations, Andean backdrop, and the Wiphala indigenous flag.

tier2.display: Sequent occupancy (Inca foundation beneath Spanish cathedral)
Catholic universalizing religion imposed by colonization
Indigenous recognition (Wiphala as official Cusco flag)
Monumental scale as colonial imperial display

tier1.display: Central plaza as public gathering space

tier2.display: Cusco as Machu Picchu tourism gateway
Spanish "Laws of the Indies" urban model (1573)

clueHighlights.clue: Rainbow-striped flag flying on Spanish colonial cathedral
Three-doorway cathedral + arcaded perimeter + central plaza
clueHighlights.inference: Spanish Laws of the Indies urban model

clueHighlights.clue: Massive stone cathedral + mountain backdrop

frq.prompt: Explain how the cultural landscape visible in this image shows BOTH colonial imposition AND indigenous identity assertion.
frq.modelAnswer: The massive Spanish Baroque cathedral with its ornate three-doorway facade represents colonial imposition of Catholic universalizing religion, built directly on top of the Inca palace Kiswarkancha as a deliberate display of religious and political dominance. At the same time, the Wiphala rainbow flag flying from the cathedral tower represents contemporary indigenous identity assertion, where the Andean peoples' flag has been officially adopted by the Cusco municipal government, reclaiming indigenous visibility on the very building that once symbolized its suppression.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Social (cultural landscape) and Political (colonial legacy and recognition) categories connecting Topic 3.2 with Topic 4.1. The Explain verb requires reasoning about how a single place can express competing political-cultural forces across centuries.

name: Hong Kong — Mong Kok Crossing
description: Busy Mong Kok street crossing showing extreme vertical density, bilingual signage, and British-legacy road infrastructure.

clueHighlights.clue: Yellow zebra crosswalk + vehicles on left
Caged balconies + window AC units on every floor
7-Eleven + Chinese character signs + mini-bus

frq.prompt: Explain how the vertical density visible in this image reflects bid-rent theory in a global financial center.
frq.modelAnswer: The image shows buildings rising many stories on both sides of the street with commercial uses at ground level and residential above, at a crossing where land values are among the highest on Earth. Bid-rent theory predicts that when land is expensive, users must maximize floor area per square meter of ground to afford the location, which produces extreme vertical growth; Hong Kong's role as a global financial center combined with its physical constraints drives bid-rent competition to an extreme, causing even residential uses to stack vertically rather than spread horizontally.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Spatial and Economic categories connecting Topic 6.3 with Topic 6.5. The Explain verb requires causal reasoning about how land values drive vertical form.

name: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia — Petaling Street
description: Covered Petaling Street market in KL's Chinatown showing Chinese cultural markers, multi-ethnic Malaysian commerce, and government-renovated tourism infrastructure.

tier1.display: "Welcome to B Hotel, Petaling Street" English banner
Motorcycle with passenger on market street

tier2.display: Monsoon rain adaptation via covered market

tier1.display: Clothing stalls with T-shirts and streetwear
Souvenir stalls with trinkets and keychains

tier2.display: SE Asian shophouse + covered market hybrid

clueHighlights.clue: Red Chinese lanterns + Chinese signage on storefronts
Crowd visibly mixing Chinese, Malay, Indian, and tourist
Red steel truss canopy over organized stalls

frq.prompt: Describe TWO visible characteristics that indicate this market is located in a multi-ethnic country with a Chinese diaspora community.
frq.modelAnswer: Red Chinese lanterns hanging overhead and Chinese-character signage on the storefronts show the presence of an established Chinese community with visible cultural markers. The crowd simultaneously contains multiple ethnic groups including Chinese-heritage, Malay, and South Asian shoppers alongside international tourists, showing that this ethnic enclave exists within a larger multi-ethnic national population rather than a monocultural one.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Social (cultural landscape) and Demographic (ethnic composition) categories connecting Topic 3.2 with Topic 3.8. The Describe verb requires naming visible characteristics without causal reasoning.

name: Tokyo, Japan — Harajuku Station area
description: Wide multi-modal street near Harajuku Station showing high-quality Japanese transportation infrastructure, urban tree canopy, and Art Nouveau-inspired street furniture.

tier1.display: Mature street trees in full leaf
Clear blue sky with cumulus clouds

clueHighlights.clue: Crisp thick crosswalk stripes + blue bicycle lane markings
Wrought-iron curvy railings + artificial-turf hoarding
Car on left side of road + horizontal traffic signal

frq.prompt: Explain how the transportation infrastructure visible in this image reflects this city's level of economic development.
frq.modelAnswer: The image shows a wide road with clearly painted zebra stripes, dedicated blue bicycle-lane markings, protected pedestrian space with decorative railings, and mature urban street trees — all maintained at very high quality. This kind of multi-modal infrastructure requires substantial and sustained public investment, which is characteristic of a core country where surplus capital supports quality-of-life urban design rather than only basic road function. The separation of vehicle, bicycle, and pedestrian zones also signals that transportation policy prioritizes non-car users, a typical pattern in highly developed cities.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Economic (development level) and Spatial (modal separation) categories connecting Topic 6.6 with Topic 7.8. The Explain verb requires causal reasoning linking infrastructure quality to development.

name: Seoul, South Korea — Insadong
description: Pedestrianized commercial street in Seoul's Insadong heritage district showing Hangul signage, art-gallery infrastructure, and modern Korean fashion retail.

tier1.display: Pink and red flower planters along walkway

clueHighlights.clue: Hangul script on multiple signs + "갤러리" gallery label
Traditional tiled roof next to modern mesh-clad building
Pedestrianized paving + planters + no cars

frq.prompt: Describe TWO visible characteristics that indicate this is a heritage district in an East Asian developed country.
frq.modelAnswer: Hangul script appears on every storefront and on a public-event banner, signaling a culturally specific Korean place. Directly alongside a modern mesh-clad commercial building stands a small building with a traditional Korean tiled roof, showing that heritage-style structures are preserved within active modern commerce — a pattern typical of a developed country that combines heritage-district regulation with ongoing commercial redevelopment.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Social (cultural landscape) and Spatial (heritage-district organization) categories connecting Topic 3.2 with Topic 6.3. The Describe verb requires naming visible characteristics without causal reasoning.

name: Dublin, Ireland — Temple Bar
description: Cobblestone pedestrian street in Dublin's Temple Bar cultural district with colorful Irish pub facades and preserved Georgian architecture.

tier2.display: Irish pub culture as central social institution
Mix of tourists and locals expected

clueHighlights.clue: Cobblestone + colorful pub facades + English signage
Georgian stone upper floors + medieval street width
Multiple pubs + cafe + hanging flower baskets + no cars

frq.prompt: Explain how the preserved historic urban form visible in this image supports the current tourism-based economy.
frq.modelAnswer: The narrow cobblestone street, colorful painted pub facades, and Georgian stone-masonry buildings have been preserved through heritage-district regulation, creating a distinctive visual character that cannot be reproduced elsewhere. Tourists are drawn to this character precisely because it is unique and legible as "Irish," which supports the dense cluster of pubs, cafes, and shops that depend on tourist footfall. The pedestrianization then reinforces the tourist economy by making the heritage character more accessible and pleasant to experience on foot.
frq.speedsConnection: Targets Political (heritage preservation) and Economic (tourism economy) categories connecting Topic 3.6 with Topic 6.3. The Explain verb requires causal reasoning linking preservation policy to economic outcomes.
